text:
Here's a document outlining the down-sizing of our company. Using this guide as a communication, craft a message to the product team informing them that we've organized tasks to aid the pausing of 3 markets in a slack channel (#product-support-for-pausing-markets), as well as organized tasks in Linear (LINK)

```untitled

Phone call to installers
Markets applicability: BE, NL, UK


Key points: 
Otovo is reorganizing its business
For a period of time now we will not be selling new projects
Every signed contract is still valid, and should be completed as planned. These projects are unaffected, and you will be paid as usual
We don’t know exactly when we will restart our sales, but we want you to be on our platform when we do
In the interim we may contact you for repair jobs in your area, either on your own installations or on others, where you will be paid like before

Partner Communications
Markets applicability: All (focus on large, commercially-meaningful partnerships)

Comms goals:
Avoid partners being surprised by our news
Make partners feel that we are in control of the situation and provide reassurance that we are a stable going concern
Make partners feel that they are top of mind for us at this time of change, reiterate our commitment to our partnerships and convey our continued desire to invest and grow together


Key Partner
Pre-changes relationship owner(s) at Otovo
Otovo person communicating
When, to whom and how
Main points

And look forward to European expansion in due course

People in Hiring Process with Us
Markets applicability: All
Timing: 11 Sep and onwards
Thank you for your interest in Otovo, your engagement with us and your patience
Otovo has announced some changes designed to align our operations with the current market dynamics and pace of installations. This is a prudent step that will help ensure we are building a sustainable business that will continue being a leading force in European residential solar, making our contribution to the tackling the energy transition challenge
As a result, at this time we are pausing our recruiting activities for this role
We look forward to staying in touch and hope to be able to have an opportunity to reengage with you in the future
Thank you again for your interest and wish you all the best in the meantime

Reactive commentary to journalists
Markets applicability: All (esp. NO and ES given Otovo’s prominence)


Otovo is taking down costs as part of a strategic decision to optimize resources and focus on areas where it can have the greatest impact. The recent past has brought jagged developments with wild swings relating to covid, the energy crisis and its aftermath, and rising interest rates. The residential solar energy market is in a cooler phase in Europe in the past two years, so this cost reduction aims to match the current demand, and allows the company to reevaluate the size and location of its staff.


Thanks to our multi-market presence across Europe, we are able to absorb any slow-down in a particular geography while benefiting from areas of growth, and our systems, processes and teams are ready for the period of recovery and renewed market growth. We believe in a rebound of the industry in the medium term and keep broadening our catalogue of products, in partnership with the top brands in the industry, so as to become the biggest market place home energy solutions in Europe.

Market Pause Plan Outline 
(UK, BE, NL)
In 2022, Otovo made a bold bet be going into 6 new markets within a single year. Some bets paid off, while others have struggled. For these, we might conclude to pause our market presence in some degree.

“Sometimes a step back is the only way forward.”

This guide serves as the outline for a plan to pause country presence.
Communication Strategy and Language Guidelines
We need to be careful and considerate when it comes to our communication around the pausing.

Our communication needs to be clear for employees, existing customers, potential customers, installers, partners and shareholders alike. We need to strike a balance between being clear that operations are stopped, while at the same time keeping the door open for our future return to the market.

Stock market communication will not mention the pausing of markets.

Final wording will have to be in local language, respecting the tone and meaning of the English language communication below.

In the relevant markets, Otovo has decided to implement a strategic pause. This will include the following changes:


We will not have an office presence in the market.
Pausing new sales after the completion of already ongoing sales.
Signing of new contracts will not take place after October 1st.
All new and ongoing projects will be completed as agreed.
After October 1st, installers can not expect to receive new Otovo projects.
Emphasize the company’s long-term commitment to other markets.
Do not:
Use the words “closed”, “suspended”, “shut down” or similar in any communication.
An FAQ is attached at the end of this document for common questions to maintain consistency across markets.
Website Updates
Our online presence in the paused markets will have to reflect the new reality.
Home Page Changes
Provide a clear but brief notice of the market pause.
Include a link to a dedicated page explaining the decision in more detail.
Provide a form to capture email addresses for a potential future re-opening of the market
My Page
Ensure that any customers who log in see a targeted message regarding the market pause..
Messaging should reassure customers that their needs will be met in the future.
Installer signup page
Provide a specific message for installers outlining the temporary halt.
Encourage installers to leave their contact information in case of a future re-opening of the market.
Otovo Installer
Provide a specific message for installers outlining the temporary halt.
Provide reassurance that ongoing projects will be completed.
Installer Communication
Draft an email for installers explaining the pause and its impact on ongoing projects.
Consider offering a Q&A session or webinar to address concerns directly.
Emphasize that the local Otovo entity will remain and that the framework agreement remains in force what regards already installed projects (warranties etc).
Sales Strategy
New interests:
As soon as possible after the announcement, the website will be closed for new leads, and no more new interests or activations will be created.
Uncontacted activations:
Lead qualifiers will not attempt to contact activations that have not been reached yet.
Planned video calls and offers sent out:
We will keep our appointments and stand by the offers that were sent out, but offer should not be set to expire later than September 30th.
Office Closures
Office presence in paused markets will be closed down. 
Office leases
While individual office leases may have some limitations, on a general basis we should not have an office presence in the relevant markets after October 1st. We seek to stop office leases as soon as possible after this date.
Legal aspects
How do we transfer bank accounts and formal roles?
Ceremony
We aim to an event to provide closure to everyone involved.
Tone of the Event:
Consider a positive, closure event that allows employees to share a final moment of connection, even in a challenging context.
Themes could focus on the journey, celebrating the effort that everyone has put in to make the market work.
Event Ideas:
"Grave beers" or a farewell gathering to mark the end, with senior leadership providing a message of thanks and optimism.
An option for a small, internal farewell event for each office.
Preferably in the office, during day time. Extended lunch, cake or something along those lines could be good. 
Communication to Leasing Customers
Markets applicability: BE, NL
Not to be used in the UK

**

Your solar subscription 

Dear customer, 
As you may have heard, Otovo is reorganizing our business in [country]. We want to assure you that this will not affect the service on your system that you signed up for. As a valued subscription customer, you will still be entitled to the same customer support, guarantees and warranties, and you can still reach us at [email] in the event of any issue with your system. 

In short: 
Your monthly subscription will continue to run as before
The service we provide you will be the same as before
There is nothing you need to do - just keep enjoying your solar panels

Best regards, 
Team Otovo

Lending banks
Restructuring to adapt to lower volumes
Reducing and optimizing marketing spend
Focus on hybrid sales model to improve conversion, already proven
Reducing # of FTEs with 160 and running more from Madrid
This will imply that the company will be cash flow breakeven at current levels
We are also reducing our focus on Belgium, Netherlands and UK as we in these countries have limited traction and we want to reduce the cash burn and complexity they bring
We will not prioritize marketing spend and have no people on the ground. We will retain the companies and an installer network required to service our leasing customers
We are communicating with the leasing customers to ensure that they understand that this will not impact them or their system
FAQs
Customer FAQs
1. Why is Otovo halting operations in this market?
Otovo is temporarily pausing operations in select markets as part of a strategic decision to optimize resources and focus on areas where we can have the greatest impact. This will help us strengthen our offerings and improve service in the long term.
2. Will my current solar installation still be supported?
Yes, all existing installations and warranties will continue to be fully supported. Our customer service team will remain available to assist with any questions or support needs.
3. Can I still place a new order?
We will stand by the offers that have been sent out already, but otherwise we are not accepting new orders in this market. We apologize for the inconvenience and hope to serve you in the future. You can sign up for updates on our website for any changes.
4. How long will this pause last? Will Otovo return to this market?
While we cannot provide a specific timeline, this is a temporary pause, not a permanent exit. We will reassess the market conditions and announce any updates as soon as possible.
5. What happens if I have an ongoing project or installation?
Any ongoing projects will continue as planned. We will work closely with our local installers to ensure your installation is completed, and you’ll receive full support during and after the process.
6. As an existing Otovo customer interested in expanding my system. What are my options?
For system expansions or upgrades, we encourage you to contact your original installer directly. You can find their contact details on your "My Page". These trusted professionals are familiar with your installation and can provide tailored advice and services.
When Otovo resumes full operations in this market, we'll be happy to assist you with any additional needs or larger projects. Feel free to sign up for updates on our website to stay informed about our return.
Installer FAQs
1. What does this pause mean for my relationship with Otovo?
We highly value our partnership and will work closely with you during this transition. While new projects may be temporarily paused, we are committed to ensuring the completion of existing installations and will honor all contractual obligations.
2. Will Otovo return to this market? Should I expect future projects?
We hope to return to this market once conditions allow, and we will communicate any updates as soon as decisions are made. While the focus is shifting for now, we look forward to potentially resuming our work together in the future.
3. How should I handle customer inquiries?
Please refer customers to Otovo's support team for any specific questions about the pause. We will provide you with a set of key messages and a point of contact should you need additional assistance in handling inquiries.
4. Will this impact payments for work already completed?
No, all payments for completed or ongoing projects will be processed as agreed. If you have any concerns, please contact your Otovo representative for support.
General Stakeholder FAQs
1. Will there be any layoffs as part of this decision?
We are evaluating staffing needs based on the operational changes. Some employees may be redeployed to other markets or teams, and we will support any transitions necessary during this period.
2. What is Otovo’s long-term plan for its business?
Otovo remains fully committed to its mission of expanding home energy solutions across Europe. This temporary pause allows us to focus our resources on markets where we can drive the most impact, and we will continue to innovate and grow in the coming years.
```



Here's a draft message for the product team:

---

Hey team! 👋

In case you're wondering, I wanted to update you on our efforts to support the pausing of 3 markets (UK, Belgium, Netherlands). We've organized tasks to help with this transition in our #product-support-for-pausing-markets Slack channel and in Linear [LINK].

Key areas we're focusing on:

1. Website Updates:
   - Home page changes
   - My Page adjustments
   - Installer signup page modifications
   - Otovo Installer platform updates

2. Communication Support:
   - Crafting messages for customers, installers, and partners
   - FAQ development for various stakeholders

3. Sales Process Adjustments:
   - Handling existing leads and activations
   - Managing ongoing projects

4. Technical Considerations:
   - Ensuring continued support for existing installations
   - Maintaining necessary infrastructure for customer service

Anything I can do to help? Not right now. We're mapping out the tasks, and will pull in people as needed. Please check the Linear board if you're curious or want to stay up-to-date. We'll be using the Slack channel for quick updates and questions.

Remember, while we're pausing operations in these markets, we're keeping the door open for future return. Let's ensure our product changes reflect this temporary nature.

If you have any questions or need clarification on anything, don't hesitate to reach out. Let's work together to make this transition as smooth as possible!

---

This message provides a concise overview of the situation, directs the team to the relevant resources, and encourages collaboration while maintaining a positive tone about the company's future prospects.
The 3rd and 4th point are a bit too vague, can you be a bit more specific? these are teammates (developers + designers) that are heavily read into the situation and domain, not high level execs.
You're right, I apologize for the vagueness. Let me revise those points to be more specific and technical for the product team:

---

Hey team! 👋

Quick update on our efforts to support the pausing of UK, Belgium, and Netherlands markets. We've organized tasks in the #product-support-for-pausing-markets Slack channel and in Linear [LINK].

Key areas we're focusing on:

1. Website Updates:
   - Home page changes: Adding market pause notice and email capture for future reopening
   - My Page adjustments: Targeted messaging for existing customers
   - Installer signup page modifications: Halting new signups, collecting contact info for future
   - Otovo Installer platform updates: Messaging about project completion and pause

2. Communication Support:
   - Crafting messages for customers, installers, and partners
   - FAQ development for various stakeholders

3. Sales Process Adjustments:
   - Disabling lead generation forms and new activation creation
   - Updating offer expiration logic to not exceed September 30th
   - Modifying the sales funnel to handle existing leads appropriately

4. Technical Considerations:
   - Maintaining customer support ticketing system for existing installations
   - Updating API endpoints to reflect paused market status
   - Adjusting backend logic for market-specific features (e.g., pricing, product availability)
   - Ensuring data retention compliance while pausing operations

We'll be using the Slack channel for quick updates and questions. The Linear board has more detailed task breakdowns if you want to dive in.

Remember, we're keeping infrastructure in place for a potential future return to these markets. Let's make sure our changes are easily reversible where possible.

Any questions or ideas? Don't hesitate to reach out. Let's make this transition smooth!

---

This revised version provides more specific, technical details that developers and designers familiar with the domain would find relevant and actionable.
summary: Product team tasks for pausing operations in UK Belgium and Netherlands
